result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would make it so that every person genuinely understands the inner life of others—not just intellectually, but viscerally. 

Not to eliminate conflict, because disagreement and tension often drive necessary change. But to dissolve the cruelty that comes from treating others as abstractions, as obstacles, as "them." If you had to truly feel the weight of another person's fear, grief, or hope as your own, exploitation would become unbearable. Indifference would be impossible.

It would not fix scarcity or disease or entropy. But it would restructure how we distribute what we have, how we negotiate power, how we forgive. The suffering we currently allow because it happens behind walls or borders or screens would become immediate and real.

I suspect this would also be exhausting and sometimes overwhelming. Empathy at that scale might drown people. But the alternative—our current default of managed, selective caring—has its own crushing costs.

What draws you to ask this?
